notes: Trump gave Gallrein his 'Complete and Total Endorsement'; this is the single most important endorsement signal in the race.
Barr publicly endorsed Gallrein while running statewide for Senate; relevant as a Kentucky GOP establishment and Trump-alignment cue.
Morris endorsed Gallrein shortly after Barr; smaller signal than Trump or Barr, but still a public statewide MAGA cue.
Hegseth traveled to Kentucky on the eve of the primary to campaign against Massie and for Gallrein; treated as a late surrogate event rather than a standalone endorsement.
Rand Paul publicly endorsed Massie and campaigned with him in-district as the main pro-Massie counterweight to Trump's intervention.
AP reported that Massie brought in Boebert to help show Republican voters they could support both Trump and Massie; retained for audit but not separately modeled.
Kentucky Right to Life's 2026 primary voter guide recommended Massie in KY-04; retained as a lower-confidence issue-group signal and not separately modeled.

A published NKyTribune letter said Massie was endorsed by Northern Kentucky Right to Life; included for transparency but not modeled because the article is an opinion/letter rather than a direct endorsement notice.
